edges.weight_note: Decreased from 9.0 — Lockheed -20.2% 1M, ITA -4.4% 1M; defense-rotation trade structurally unwinding under crisis-relief regime

edges.rationale: April CPI 3.8% YoY (BLS May 12, three-year high) with energy contributing >40% of headline gain = first cycle inflation print to fully reflect oil pass-through; closes near-term Fed easing door. Fed cannot cut while Brent >$100.

edges.weight_note: Decreased from 9.5 — Russia uranium-custodian track sidelined per Peskov May 5; Pakistan track primary

edges.rationale: Mirror of india->uae. ~3.5M Indian diaspora in UAE makes any anti-UAE framing politically unworkable for Indian press; Modi UAE first leg cements bilateral despite combatant disclosure.

Trump May 5 Truth Social explicitly cited Pakistan's request as driver of Project Freedom pause; Trump May 7-8 publicly disclosed Pakistan asked US 'not to militarily engage Iran during negotiations' — first public Trump description of Pakistan as active diplomatic constraint on US military operations.

E2 from markets.md: CF -6.5% / NTR -4.4% on May 7 is direct MOU peace-dividend pricing via Hormuz reopening expectation. April 17 playbook (CF -9.6% on prior Hormuz reopening signal) confirms repeatable causal mechanism. Initial weight 6.0 (high directness, confirmed twice in cycle).

E4 from markets.md: AI-cycle decoupling persistent over 30+ days; Apple-Samsung-Intel chip-talks halo continues to rotate flows to Korea/Taiwan/US tech NOT Indian IT services. KOSPI +37.3% / TAIEX +26.2% / SMH +37.5% on month vs Nifty IT structurally underperforming. INVERSE relationship — indian-it loses flow share when semiconductors rallies.

Saudi covert kinetic disclosure (Newsweek+WSJ late-March strikes) moves Saudi from 'distancing from UAE military track' to 'partial convergence' with UAE's confirmed combatant role; structural Gulf-row re-encoding.

E1 from markets.md: Brent collapsed -7% May 6 → Lockheed Martin -18.1% on the month while ITA +3.7% today is aerospace-led, not defense-led. Strong inverse correlation between oil-war-premium and pure-play defense names confirmed across 3-month window.
France's Charles de Gaulle CSG transited Suez to Red Sea/Gulf of Aden following CMA CGM San Antonio strike — first European naval-presence shift of cycle. Macron called for Hormuz reopening.
Macron-Pezeshkian call confirmed; CMA CGM strike (CBS-attributed to Iran, IRGC has NOT claimed) is implicit France-Iran kinetic exchange even though Macron leveraged Maltese-flag technicality.
CMA CGM (world's third-largest container line, French-owned) vessel struck May 5 in Hormuz.
French CSG deployment could over time depress AWRP if European naval presence reduces transit risk perception.
France's bifurcated posture — CSG deployment + Macron-Pezeshkian call — is European autonomy-positioning rather than US-tail-wagging; UK-French conditional Hormuz mission separate but parallel.

Pezeshkian May 7-8 public claim of 2.5-hour face-to-face meeting with Mojtaba (CLAIMED — no date/location/witnesses/visual) operates against same-day CNN/KVIA US intel assessment that Mojtaba is alive 'shaping strategy' but 'out of public view.' Stage 2 narrative-management playbook (named senior officials publicly attesting to private contact without evidence).

E6 from markets.md: Korean fabs source 65% helium from Qatar with 6-12 week buffer per Smith/Discovery Alert; Samsung first to bite if disruption persists past mid-June. The most TIME-SENSITIVE chokepoint in graph.

E7 from markets.md: Lockheed -18.8% MoM + ITA -3.6% MoM directly attributable to ceasefire/MOU rotation reversal despite May 7 IRGC destroyer attacks + May 8 second tanker disabling + Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' speech. Markets choosing diplomatic-track narrative over trinary-regime operational reality. Cleanest 'ceasefire takes the war premium out' signal in cycle.

E8 from markets.md: Frontline +13.5% MoM, BOAT +18.6% 3M, VLCC TCE Q1 $107K vs Q4 $74K, Baltic Exchange TD3C briefly WS408. Multiple consistent activations. Now the most reliable 'trinary regime' tracker after AWRP.

Saudi Al-Hadath May 8 denial of WSJ scoop on offensive-airspace lift + public endorsement of 'Pakistan's efforts to broker a peace agreement' = MBS playbook of operational deniability + public alignment with Iranian-negotiating-side mediation track. Soft-mediator posture activated.

A11 from intel.md: Bahrain MoI announced May 9 it had arrested 41 alleged IRGC-linked individuals — first major Gulf-state internal action against alleged IRGC network during the cycle. Bahrain hosts US Fifth Fleet. Signals openly bifurcated Gulf response: Bahrain (pro-US, anti-IRGC), Saudi (deniable-pro-US-operationally + public-pro-mediation), UAE (hawkish operationally), Qatar (mediating with IRGC contacts), Oman (silent), Kuwait (denied airspace). Two readings: substantive intel breakthrough OR opportunistic crackdown using war as pretext (rights-group framing).

B1 from intel.md: Sunday May 10 bulk carrier strike 23nm NE of Doha — first commercial vessel attack since CMA CGM May 5 (also unclaimed) and FIRST near Qatar's coast. Operationally consistent with IRGC signal to Qatar that mediator role does not buy operational immunity for blockade-aligned shipping; pressure-tactic to set price for Qatari mediation outcome. Concurrent with Qatari PM al-Thani's Miami mediation meeting. NO CLAIM OF RESPONSIBILITY (consistent with May 5 'claim-management discrimination pattern'). Three reading frames operationally consistent: (i) IRGC signal to Qatar; (ii) Houthi-style proxy operation (LOW probability, wrong geometry); (iii) misfire/accidental engagement. Conservative 7.0 weight reflects unclaimed/CONTESTED-perpetrator status.

A10 from intel.md: IRGC strike on PDKI Girde Chal Kurdish camp north of Erbil (REPORTED — FDD's Long War Journal + Hengaw, single-side Kurdish/diaspora frame). Second Kurdish-camp strike in 5 days after May 5 Komala Sourdash strike. Tactically distinct from kinetic exchanges with US/Israeli forces; targets Iranian opposition exile groups. Confirms IRGC offensive air operations remain unimpeded by US enforcement. Conservative 4.0 weight on REPORTED single-side framing.

B4 from intel.md: UAE Defense Ministry confirmed May 8 that 3 people were wounded after air defenses engaged 2 ballistic missiles + 3 drones launched by Iran toward the UAE (UPDATED — yesterday's brief understated). SECOND major UAE-targeted Iranian attack of cycle (first: May 4 Fujairah refinery strike). UAE now demonstrably on IRGC operational targeting list.

Per Axios scoop: 'While Pakistan has been the official mediator since the beginning of the war, the Qataris have been working behind the scenes... White House views them as especially effective in negotiations with Iran. Qataris functioning as one of at least three back channels.' Pakistan retains OFFICIAL/political mediation; Qatar is the SUBSTANTIVE Iran-side operational channel via IRGC senior generals. Edge captures the parallel-track structure (not adversarial; complementary).

B6 from intel.md: WSJ May 11 revelation (REPORTED via single primary, multi-amplified) that UAE Air Force secretly carried out airstrikes inside Iran during the war, including April 8 strike on Lavan Island oil refinery using Mirage 2000-9 (55,000 bpd capacity offline months). UAE moves from passive US host to ONLY third country to join US-Israel kinetic operations against Iran. Edge captures the covert military co-combatant relationship — UAE has not publicly acknowledged; Iran silent on WSJ exposure. Conservative 8.0 weight on REPORTED single-primary with strong multi-amplification.

B6 from intel.md: WSJ revealed UAE conducted offensive strikes on Iran's Lavan refinery; 'Washington reportedly positively received Abu Dhabi's participation.' Edge captures the operational co-belligerent relationship vs the prior 'financial/security architecture host' framing. Iran's prior UAE-targeted strikes now retroactively readable as direct retaliation against confirmed third combatant.

Saudi visibly distancing from UAE military track in light of WSJ Lavan revelation. Saudi denied via Al-Hadath May 8 that it allowed offensive airspace use. The Gulf-state response now openly fractured: UAE (covert third combatant), Saudi (deniable-operational + public-pro-mediation, distancing), Qatar (substantive mediator), Bahrain (US-aligned counter-IRGC), Kuwait (denied airspace), Oman (silent).

Per markets dossier E4: AWRP ~1% H&M (8x pre-war baseline) is now a direct mechanism in the Brent floor — confirmed via Lloyd's List + S&P Global + LMA. Promotes from 2nd-order to 1st-order edge as confirmed price-formation mechanism.

CF Industries is world's largest publicly-traded nitrogen-fertilizer producer; +8.2% 1D May 12 = MOVE OF THE DAY. CF, Nutrien, BASF, Linde all bid together = sector regime shift, not single-stock event. Q1 EBITDA $983M / 99% ammonia capacity utilization confirms pricing-power capture.
Qatar/Ras Laffan QAFCO + SABIC Agri-Nutrients curtailed since Feb 28 = ~35-40% global urea export capacity affected. CF directly captures the displaced supply via US Gulf urea +50%.

Modi May 10-11 'cut fertilizer 50%' appeal = Indian-government public confirmation binding shock. India IPL DAP tender May 11 received offers $935/t cfr (normal $580-650) = extraordinary print. UBS raised CF target $97→$140 explicitly citing severity of disruptions.
KOSPI is the primary Korean equity benchmark. -1.7% 1D first profit-taking session of cycle after +33.1% 1M / +78% YTD vertical run.
Samsung +111% YTD / SK Hynix +144% YTD = dominant weights in KOSPI; AI infrastructure CapEx demand cycle.
Korean fab buffer ~4 weeks remaining to mid-June exhaustion = most time-sensitive trigger in graph. Tier-1 chipmaker production-halt risk.
HMM Namu forensics May 11 — Seoul stopped short of publicly blaming Iran (mine + torpedo ruled out; 2 unidentified aerial objects). Korea moved from passive helium victim to active diplomatic actor.
FII -₹8,437.56 cr May 11 (highest since April 24); YTD CY26 ₹2.06 lakh cr exceeds full-year 2025 by ₹40k cr. FII is the dominant transmission mechanism in every India-market causal chain.
FPI ownership ~16% (2-decade low); DII absorbing ~70-90% of FII selling but gap widening. Nifty -1.49% May 11 directly attributable to FII flow.
FII outflow ₹2.06 lakh cr YTD drives USD demand on capital flight; INR ₹95.54 fresh record breaking RBI ₹95.50 threshold directly linked.
RBI reserves -$7.79bn week ended May 1 = active intervention. NOP cap + NDF restriction operational since May 5 to slow FII-driven dollar demand.
Iran's May 10-11 counter-proposal contained first HEU concession of cycle. Nuclear file is now central diplomatic axis of cycle.
Trump publicly rejected Iran HEU concession with return clause as 'TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE.' New OFAC sanctions May 12 targeted Iranian nuclear research with military applications.

Netanyahu CBS 60 Minutes May 11: war 'not over' until uranium removed + enrichment sites dismantled — 'you go in and you take it out.' Maximalist position raises ceiling on what counts as a US-Iran deal.

Russia (Putin May 9 standing offer) is implicit third-country candidate for HEU transfer per Iranian counter-proposal terms, but never publicly named. If formally named, uranium-sector + russia + nuclear-program all rerate.
URA +3.7% 1D / +12.3% 1M reflects markets pricing nuclear-energy-security premium as durable. Uranium decoupled from gold (-0.7% 1M) and oil (+6.3% 1M).

Trump Trump-Xi summit Day 1 May 14: Trump PUBLICLY OPENED HEU-via-China custodian door — 'only Washington or Beijing can retrieve Iran's nuclear material if Tehran agrees to hand it over' (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — ToI primary). Material policy signal substitutes China for Russia as proposed HEU custodian. Weight bumped 9.0 → 9.5 on policy-shift confirmation + multi-source corroboration.

Trump-Xi Summit Day 2 produced joint statement: Iran 'can never have a nuclear weapon' + Strait of Hormuz 'must remain open' (CONFIRMED — ToI May 15 liveblog primary, CBS, CNN, China MFA readout, Al Jazeera). Both sides committed to 'constructive China-U.S. relationship of strategic stability' framework guiding next three years. China MFA preserves Iran's 'legitimate right to peaceful use of nuclear energy' escape valve. Weight bumped 9.5 → 10.0 on joint-statement issuance (operational rhetorical alignment achieved, though operational custodian framework not yet announced).

Modi-MBZ Abu Dhabi meeting May 15: LPG + Strategic Petroleum Reserve MoUs to be signed; Fujairah expansion alternative-route plan publicly disclosed (CONFIRMED — Business Standard + The Tribune + Khaleej Times + The National + DD News). UAE Minister Sultan Al Jaber: 'energy has been weaponised by Hormuz being taken hostage effectively' = cleanest Gulf-state public weaponisation framing of Iran. Alternative-route engineering as Hormuz-bypass strategy publicly disclosed for the first time. Weight bumped 8.5 → 9.0 on operational alternative-route plan + MoUs.

Iran's ambassador to China publicly framed Beijing as 'political balancing' partner — 'China had paved the way for previous direct U.S.-Iran peace talks in Islamabad'; 'predicted Beijing could emerge as a key player in ongoing diplomacy' (Tasnim Iranian state primary + CBS amplification CONFIRMED RHETORIC). Publicly-sanctioned Iranian state-media-side scaffolding for China-mediation arc — institutional ground deliberately laid for HEU-via-China structure Trump opened in Beijing. Diplomatic architecture pivot from Russia-track-stalled to China-track-opening visible from BOTH sides.

Iran FM Araghchi used Delhi BRICS-FM platform to directly threaten UAE: 'Iranian FM threatens UAE, claims Tehran knew of Netanyahu's visit to Abu Dhabi' (ToI May 14 liveblog primary CONFIRMED RHETORIC). UAE has not publicly responded; UAE state-aligned media institutional silence continues. Iran-UAE conflict surface remains active third-combatant relationship per WSJ Lavan disclosure.

Reuters May 12-13 cited 'two Western officials and two Iranian officials' (Reuters primary; Times of Israel + Haaretz + JNS + RT + US News + Pravda secondary) — materially strengthens basis from one-cluster (Newsweek-WSJ yesterday) to multi-cluster (WSJ + Reuters independent primary scoops). Reuters adds: 'Saudi Arabia made Iran aware of the strikes... led to an understanding between the two countries to de-escalate.' Saudi-Iran/UAE-Iran trajectories bifurcating: Riyadh paid price of engagement and exited; Abu Dhabi remains operationally engaged. Saudi-strikes remain REPORTED (not CONFIRMED — Saudi/Iran institutionally silent); basis upgrade justifies graph weight bump 9.0 → 9.5.

Lloyd's List May 11 reports VLCC TD3C 'in unchartered territory' at $420-462K/day vs graph's last-recorded ~$100K = MAJOR ESCALATION SIGNAL. Paper-Brent at $105.68 vs spot VLCC at $420K is the cleanest paper-physical divergence indicator. Per markets dossier E: 1st-order edge (direct mechanism — tanker rate IS the market's assessment of Hormuz risk); source: Lloyd's List primary + Baltic Exchange. Initial weight 9.0; figure flagged for Baltic Exchange cross-verification before propagating to other downstream edges.

IDF announced May 13 it carried out a 'weeklong raid' beyond the Litani River near Zawtar al-Sharqiyah led by the Golani Brigade's Reconnaissance Unit (CONFIRMED — ToI liveblog May 13 + JNS + Haaretz). 'Some 15 Hezbollah operatives' killed past 24h; 'some 45 Hezbollah targets' total over 24h. Meaningful upgrade from yesterday's 'first publicly-acknowledged Litani crossing.' State Department CONFIRMED primary: third round US-facilitated Israel-Lebanon talks Washington May 14-15 (Karam-Dermer); April-23-extended 3-week ceasefire expires May 15 as talks finish — choreographed.

Per markets E4: confirm uranium-sector as first-class node; add edge to nuclear-program (proposed). Initial weight 8.0 reflects URA pricing of nuclear-diplomacy events (HEU news, custodian announcements, enrichment-strike threats).

VIX promoted to first-class node per markets E1. VIX 17.99 (-2.1%) as summit-pause digital = key transmission mechanism for binary event-window pricing. Initial weight 8.0 reflects VIX as event-window proxy for S&P direction around discrete kinetic-decision windows.
VIX prices forcible delay of decision (Trump-in-Beijing pause), not threat universe. Iran-related kinetic events repricer of VIX through equity-market channel.
VIX -2.1% paradox correlates with Brent retrace to $106.29 below $108-110 trigger zone — both pricing summit pause as forced kinetic ceiling May 14-16.
VIX 17.99 prices NO escalation through Trump-Xi summit + modest probability beyond. Single tweet from Beijing or single missile launch reprices everything within same session = binary digital.
Mirror of vix->sp-500. VIX behaviour now central to graph because it operationalises the kinetic vs paper-disruption-vs-de-escalation trinary in a tradeable way.

Samsung Electronics + SK Hynix = 42.2% of KOSPI weight (record concentration). Samsung intraday -5.4% May 15 from prior-day all-time high contributed materially to KOSPI -6.1% (single largest equity move of cycle).

Samsung's HeRS helium-recycling system operational but covers only ~18.6% of consumption; Korea sources 64.7% from Qatar. Helium-clock-expiry window May 16-23 begins tomorrow with Samsung at the center of the supply-side stack.

Samsung 18-day strike planned May 21-June 7 (CONFIRMED — Bloomberg + Korean press). Full-stoppage projection ~1 trillion won/day damage. PM Kim Min-seok emergency ministerial meeting. April one-day strike caused 18% memory fab output drop, 58% foundry output drop per shift.

SK Hynix has publicly claimed 'diversified supplies' on helium (CLAIMED — one-sided, NOT independently corroborated; TrendForce data-driven analysis is REPORTED). Capacity 'essentially zero' through 2027-2030.

SK Hynix is the dominant HBM supplier for AI accelerators; Nvidia took the lion's share of 2026 output. Available capacity 'essentially zero' per insider reporting (Tom's Hardware + Wccftech + TradingKey + Bloomberg). Alphabet/Meta/Microsoft/Nvidia/Amazon/Google making unprecedented financing proposals to fund dedicated production lines and ASML EUV lithography machines.

Modi-MBZ Abu Dhabi meeting May 15: LPG + Strategic Petroleum Reserve MoUs to be signed; Fujairah expansion alternative-route plan publicly disclosed for first time. India and UAE explored (a) Indian vessels loading more oil and LPG from Fujairah, (b) expansion of Fujairah oil terminal capacity, (c) UAE laying additional cross-peninsula pipeline.

Hui Chuan seized 38nm NE of Fujairah May 14 (Honduras-flagged, bound for Iranian waters per UKMTO advisory; perpetrator unattributed; NO claim of responsibility). Cycle's second confirmed Iranian-direction seizure on a vessel previously at anchor in UAE waters.

Akraminia (Artesh spokesperson, IRNA) + Hassanzadeh (IRGC drill complete) synchronised kinetic-readiness messaging across both Iranian military pillars on the same day Trump-Xi joint statement said Iran 'can never have a nuclear weapon.'

UAE Minister Sultan Al Jaber publicly framed Hormuz situation as 'weaponised by Iran' — cleanest Gulf-state public language of cycle. Modi-MBZ Fujairah expansion plan publicly disclosed = operational alternative-route engineering as Hormuz-bypass strategy.

MSV Haj Ali (Indian-flagged dhow) sunk off Oman May 13 in suspected drone attack (CONFIRMED sinking; CLAIMED attribution — Iran not confirmed as attacker). India MEA called the attack 'unacceptable' — strongest public Indian condemnation of the cycle. Tag floor: REPORTED on Iranian attribution; CONFIRMED on India MEA condemnation language.

Staff Sgt. Negev Dagan, 20, Golani Brigade 12th Battalion killed by Hezbollah mortar fire near Litani May 14 (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel primary, JNS, i24News, Haaretz, Israeli MoD primary). KIA trigger added yesterday at Day 1 of 14 fired on Day 2. Mortar fire distinct from prior drone-attrition pattern = transition to ground-engagement casualty regime confirmed.

Iran allowed 30+ Chinese ships to transit since May 13 night under 'Iranian management protocols' (REPORTED — Fars/IRGC-Navy senior official primary; Iran state TV; Xinhua; Al Jazeera; CBS; Defense Post; Newsweek). Defense Post and Newsweek noted Iran has occasionally allowed Chinese ships even during the broader blockade because China is the largest buyer of Iranian oil. Promotes China-Hormuz from import_dependency to selective_chinese_transit instrument.

Per markets.md E1: Warsh confirmed 54-45 May 13 + April PPI +1.4% m/m (biggest since Dec 2022) + 10Y +14bp to 4.595% (highest since Feb 2025). Markets uncertain about Warsh reaction function for first 90 days = term premium expanding. New cascade edge proposed in markets.md.

Per markets.md E1: Warsh + PPI + Trump-Iran reversal three-force collision drove S&P -1.24%, NASDAQ -1.54%, Dow -1.07%. Stagflation regime shift session — cleanest single example of three independent forces colliding in one tape.
Samsung + SK Hynix = 42.2% of KOSPI cap-weighted. Samsung -8.6% Friday. JPMorgan loss estimate up to ₩43T ($28.8B) — materially larger than ~₩1T/day public anchor.
April one-day strike caused 18% drop in memory fab output, 58% drop in foundry output on affected shift. JPMorgan ₩43T worst-case. Samsung-SK Hynix-helium triple supply-side stack.
TTF €49.86 +4.63% third consecutive daily increase, highest since April. Europe no longer in 'alarm nor relief' mode; ECB Lagarde 'new energy shock' framework operative.

Per markets.md E4 strengthened: AWRP 3-8% of vessel value ($3-8M/transit) vs 0.1-0.15% pre-war = 20-50x; Hormuz traffic ~30 vessels/day vs 138 pre-war; some transits paying 10% per Lloyd's Market Association. Weight raised from 6.0 to 10.0 reflecting direct mechanism confirmed every day + StanChart $130-145 path activation.
Node creation 2026-05-18. Barakah Nuclear Energy Plant is operated by UAE state nuclear corporation (ENEC/Nawah Energy) at Al Dhafra.

Per markets dossier Section E: May 17 drone strike on Barakah perimeter — first nuclear-infrastructure kinetic incident of cycle. Attribution UNCLAIMED but operationally consistent with Iran-aligned proxy (likely Iran-aligned Iraqi militia or other deniable channel). Initial weight 7.0 (1st-order kinetic, nuclear-symbolic). IAEA 'grave concern' primary.

Markets dossier C1: Barakah strike is one of the principal drivers of Monday Brent +2.0% to $111.47 (StanChart $130-145 path activates — 2-session sustain rule met). Nuclear-infrastructure-adjacent attack adds a categorically new escalation tier to the cycle's risk-premium architecture.

First nuclear-infrastructure-adjacent kinetic incident of the cycle. IAEA 'grave concern' primary statement. Inverts the strategic premise that nuclear infrastructure had been kept off the kinetic target list during the war.

Per markets dossier Section E + intel B1: May 17 — 3 drones launched from Iraqi airspace intercepted over Saudi airspace per Brig al-Maliki. First confirmed Saudi territorial breach of cycle. Iraqi government has not claimed responsibility; PMU/Kata'ib Hezbollah/Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq are most likely vectors by capability and prior pattern (same militias named in WSJ April 21 framing). Iran → Iraqi militia → Saudi territory = 2nd-order directness; initial weight 6.0.
